## 1. Macro / Rates / Inflation

**Fed held benchmark rate at 3.5%-3.75% in January 2026**, pausing after three consecutive cuts in 2025. Market sees low odds of March cut; most expect Fed to hold at March 18 meeting.

**February 2026 CPI: +0.3% monthly, +2.4% annually (in line with expectations); core +0.2% monthly, +2.5% annually**. Annualized inflation rate recently decreased to 2.4% in January.

**10-year Treasury yield climbed to 4.19% on March 11**, catalyzed by February CPI report and geopolitical tensions. Spread between 2-year and 10-year Treasury widened significantly, suggesting bear steepening.

**Materiality: MEDIUM** | **Sleeve: core_equity, monetary_hedges** | **Signal: CONFIRMS**  
The Fed's pause and elevated yields confirm the existing momentum signal framework. Real rates rising would typically pressure growth assets, but oil spike tied to Iran war may drive "stagflationary" scenario where inflation remains sticky while growth risks mount. Current VTI momentum (rank 9/18) and precious metals strength (IAUM rank 5, SIVR rank 2) align with this macro backdrop.

## 2. Geopolitical / Commodity

**U.S.-Israel military conflict with Iran entered 12th day on March 11, following Operation "Midnight Hammer" launched February 28, 2026**. Strait of Hormuz effectively paralyzed by naval mines and drone threats; ~20% of world's oil supply flows through this route; shipping insurance canceled for Persian Gulf.

Higher oil prices complicate inflation outlook; crude briefly topped $100/barrel Monday, up ~4% in Wednesday trading. Oil sustaining above $110 could trigger "stagflationary" recession if Strait remains closed.

**VIX rose above 25 on March 11**, suggesting market bracing for daily S&P 500 swings of ~1.5%, turbulence not seen since Russia-Ukraine onset.

**Materiality: HIGH** | **Sleeve: defense_energy, monetary_hedges** | **Signal: CONFIRMS**  
XLE (rank 10/18), ITA (rank 8/18), IAUM (rank 5/18), SIVR (rank 2/18) all showing positive momentum. Geopolitical stress directly supports defense/energy and precious metals allocations. Oil spike is priced into current momentum but Strait closure risk remains unresolved.

## 3. AI / Semiconductor

**Bloomberg reports U.S. regulators drafted rules requiring government approval to ship AI chips anywhere outside U.S.**, giving Commerce Department control over AMD and Nvidia exports globally. Proposal would turn Commerce Department into gatekeeper for every large-scale AI infrastructure buildout globally.

**BIS revised license review policy for certain semiconductors to China/Macau**, changing from presumption of denial to case-by-case review for Nvidia H200 and less advanced chips (effective January 15, 2026). China announced new AI/semiconductor subsidy program after Bloomberg report to reduce dependence on Nvidia/AMD hardware.

**Antitrust enforcement in AI space has "permanent seat at table"** under Trump administration, coupled with focus on preventing "viewpoint discrimination".

**Materiality: MEDIUM** | **Sleeve: ai_tech** | **Signal: CONTRADICTS**  
SOXQ (rank 7/18), CHAT (rank 6/18) both positive but mid-pack. DTCR (rank 14/18) lagging. Small deployments (<1,000 GPUs) face lightest burden; friction concentrates at hyperscaler 10k-200k+ GPU range. Export control tightening is structural headwind for AI infrastructure buildout, but momentum scores reflect this as already-priced. No override recommended — signals capture the news.

## 4. Energy / Materials

**January 2026: uranium spot climbed 24% in single month to $101.26/lb**, best monthly performance in years. Northshore Global Uranium Mining Index (URNMX) surged 39.5% YTD, Nasdaq Sprott Junior Uranium Miners Index +45.25%.

**Trump administration Section 232 proclamation (January 2026) declared uranium imports national security risk**, empowering Commerce Secretary to pursue import restrictions, price floors, potential equity stakes in domestic miners. Alongside $80B pledge for AP1000 reactor construction and $2.7B DOE enrichment funding.

**TerraPower received construction license for Natrium reactor in March 2026**, beginning construction "within weeks" at Kemmerer, Wyoming site. China's Linglong One SMR scheduled to begin commercial operations in first half 2026, world's first commercial onshore SMR.

**Materiality: HIGH** | **Sleeve: strategic_materials** | **Signal: CONFIRMS**  
URNM (rank 1/18 — top momentum), COPX (rank 3/18), REMX (rank 11/18) all in strategic_materials sleeve. Uranium policy shift and nuclear buildout directly support thesis. URNM's #1 ranking validated by regulatory catalyst and supply tightness.

**FDA approved 46 new medicines in 2025** (below 2023-24 but ahead of historical norms); Commissioner Marty Makary streamlining some areas but "revolving door" and layoffs created uncertainty, "slipping" timelines. FDA on-time approval rate slipped to ~75% in final quarters of 2025.

## 7. Rates / Currency

**Real yield on 10-year TIPS averaged 1.97% in 2025**, edging up to highest since 2007; gold rose >65% in 2025 despite this, marking sharpest gain since 1979. Dollar gold-TIPS correlation broke down since Russia invasion 2022: median r-coefficient +0.02 vs -0.73 (2003-2021).

**J.P. Morgan forecasts gold averaging $5,055/oz by Q4 2026, rising to $5,400/oz by end of 2027**. Core CPI remains above Fed 2% target near 3.5%; as long as inflation outpaces Treasury yields, real rates stay negative — most bullish condition for gold.

**Materiality: MEDIUM** | **Sleeve: monetary_hedges** | **Signal: CONFIRMS**
